desc: Beirut is a Beirut Governorate, Mediterranean coast base shaped by corniche seaside walks, armenian and levantine food scenes, national museum antiquities. It suits food-led city breaks and rewards timing around the local weather curve, because crowding, room rates and transfer friction shift noticeably by season.
hls: Day trips to Byblos and mountains

todo.name: Beirut Corniche
todo.desc: Walk from Raouche toward the marina for sea air, people-watching and sunset light.

todo.name: National Museum of Beirut
todo.desc: Use the museum to frame Lebanon's deep history before taking day trips north or inland.

todo.name: Mar Mikhael and Gemmayzeh
todo.desc: Explore restaurants, bars and galleries while checking current opening patterns locally.

todo.name: Byblos day trip
todo.desc: Add the coast north of Beirut for harbour ruins, old souks and a different pace.

wifi.note: Typical visitor connectivity in Beirut is usable around central stays, but backup mobile data is wise for day trips and smaller guesthouses.

prac.fltNote: Beirut uses one main airport close to the city; route availability and fares can shift quickly with regional conditions, so check schedules before committing.
prac.currency: Lebanese pound (LBP); US dollars are also widely used in some travel contexts
prac.lang: Arabic; French and English are common in Beirut and tourism services
prac.visa: UK/Irish passport holders should verify Lebanon visa-on-arrival and security-related entry rules before travel.
bestFor: Day trips with a private driver
alerts: Monitor official travel advice and local security conditions.
Traffic can make short airport distances slow.
Power cuts and infrastructure disruption can affect some stays.

sweet: May, Jul and Oct are the most balanced months for weather comfort, trip logistics and destination atmosphere.
